summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of the legendary Watkins Glen ’73 soundcheck, dominated by a soaring Jam that weaves Bird Song, Wharf Rat and an early Fire on the Mountain tease.
review: The Miller‑sourced soundboard upgrade delivers exceptional clarity, letting listeners hear every nuance of the famed Watkins Glen soundcheck.  The performance shines most brightly in the extended jam – praised for its Keith electric solo, a Bird Song segment and a Wharf Rat climax that even hints at Fire on the Mountain.  While the surrounding song renditions are described as routine, the jam alone makes this recording a must‑hear.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both musical performance (especially the jam) and audio fidelity across >10 detailed reviews.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers note this as a major upgrade over previous sources – crisp, clear, well‑balanced with no noticeable dropouts or muffling.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A brief soundcheck turned impromptu concert; the set is loose but builds to an epic, tightly‑played jam that defines the recording.

band_member_comments.Phil: Described as "absolutely insane" by a reviewer, highlighting his energetic bass work during the jam.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's electric piano solo is repeatedly praised – he “taps into his electric” and drives the 13‑minute improvisation.
